relevant_files.role: Orchestrates scanning of PR diffs, currently line-by-line.
Regex-based pattern engine using LazyLock and patterns.json.
Database of 14 security patterns with regex, severity, and confidence.
Core data structures for Finding, PatternDefinition, and Severity.
Integration tests documenting current multi-line limitations.

conventions.commits: Conventional commits (feat, test, docs) observed in issue/PR history.
conventions.testing: Integration tests in tests/ directory, unit tests in src/ modules using AAA pattern.
conventions.linting: Strict workspace lints: unsafe_code=warn, unused_must_use=deny, clippy::pedantic=warn.
conventions.error_handling: Uses thiserror for libraries and anyhow for applications/CLI.

related_issues.title: feat(security): evaluate tree-sitter for AST-based vulnerability detection
related_issues.relevance: Primary issue: target for tree-sitter evaluation.

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Documents the architectural gap tree-sitter aims to fill.

related_issues.title: test(security): add negative test documenting multi-line detection limitation
related_issues.relevance: Merged PR implementing the limitation test case.

constraints: READ-ONLY: No code changes or commits allowed.
Hybrid approach: Keep regex for simple patterns, add tree-sitter for data-flow.
Language support: Each language requires a specific tree-sitter grammar crate.
Performance: Must maintain fast scanning for PR diffs.
test_coverage: 4 integration tests in security_integration.rs covering SQLi, secrets, safe patterns, and multi-line limitations.

library_findings.notes: Core library for AST construction and pattern querying.
Language-specific grammar for Rust AST parsing.

approaches.name: Hybrid AST-Regex Scanner
approaches.description: Introduce an AstScanner alongside the existing PatternEngine. Use tree-sitter for complex multi-line patterns (SQLi, Command Injection) while keeping regex for simple single-line patterns (Secrets, Debug flags).
approaches.pros: Maintains performance for simple cases; enables multi-line detection; lower migration risk.
approaches.cons: Increased architectural complexity; dual pattern definition formats (JSON regex vs S-expression queries).

approaches.name: Tree-sitter Query Engine
approaches.description: Migrate all 14 patterns to tree-sitter queries. Replace the regex engine entirely with a query-based system that operates on the full file content instead of line-by-line diffs.
approaches.pros: Unified detection logic; significantly reduced false positives; superior language awareness.
approaches.cons: High effort to rewrite all patterns; binary size increase from multiple grammars; requires full file context for diff scanning.

recommendation: Implement the Hybrid AST-Regex Scanner. Start with tree-sitter-rust to resolve the documented SQLi limitation in #735, then incrementally add grammars for other high-impact languages. This balances detection power with binary size and implementation effort.
